cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The question is about what we can infer from the headline "Safina goes from basket case to top of the world." The options are A to D. First, I need to remember what the article says. The headline is from the Sydney Morning Herald during the Australian Open. The article mentions that Safina is emotionally explosive, like her brother Marat. The headline was used to describe her volatile emotional state, "mad as a snake." The phrase "basket case" usually means someone who is very nervous or unstable emotionally. So the headline is probably saying that she started off being very emotional or unstable (basket case) and then rose to the top (top of the world). Looking at the options: A) She wanted to top the world when she was a baby in a basket. That doesn't make sense because "basket case" isn't literal here. It's an idiom. B) After winning the Australian Open, she cried. The article mentions she made it to the Australian Open final, but the headline isn't specifically about crying after winning. The phrase "basket case" refers to emotional instability, not necessarily crying after a win. C) Too nervous at first but gained confidence and won. This seems possible. If she started as a basket case (nervous, unstable) and then became top of the world (successful), the transformation would be from nervous to confident. D) Learnt from the basket case. The term "basket case" here isn't referring to someone she learned from but describes her own state. So D doesn't make
